QF.id: VI Depository and its Business Partners_59

question.text: A DP's internal auditor must certify AML policy compliance in which report?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In every quarterly report, the internal auditor must check and certify whether the Participant has complied with the AML Policy. Any deficiencies should be specifically pointed out. **The Trap:** 'Half-yearly audit report only' covers the general depository operations audit, but AML compliance certification is specifically quarterly. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV covers AML audit requirements for market intermediaries. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three AML certifications: (1) Initial certification that AML policy exists (one-time), (2) Quarterly auditor certification of compliance, (3) Half-yearly Compliance Certificate by the Compliance Officer.
